message: I’m here to provide information about how to find housing.  It’s a good idea to think about housing early on.
My sister wanted to live in residence when she went to college but by the time she applied, all the spots were filled and there was a long waitlist.
She had to scramble to find something off campus.
It was. Luckily, she found a room to rent, but she lives with 5 other roommates and her rent is pretty expensive.
I don’t want that to happen to me.
How long ahead should I start looking for housing when getting ready to start postsecondary?
If you want to live on campus students should start applying for campus housing early, at the time of applying to the postsecondary institution.
What if I decide to live somewhere else outside the school?

Same thing. The earlier you start looking for housing the better. Many people think they can find a place right as or just before the classes start and end up having a hard time finding a place. It can be competitive.
Oh wow! Now that would be a big problem.
How do I find out more about my housing options?
The postsecondary institution website will have a page on housing. It will list housing options, both on-campus and around the city.
How do I know which option is a good fit for me?
This pamphlet provides more detailed information about housing options. It also provides some pros and cons about your options that may help guide your decision-making.
